relocation.package_summary: unknown — I could not verify a role-specific Databricks relocation package from public sources. Public benefits pages confirm broad employee benefits but do not publish relocation amounts, broker model, lease-break coverage, temp housing, or tax gross-up terms.
relocation.policy_notes: Databricks public careers/benefits materials do not disclose relocation policy details; confirm with recruiter whether this role is remote, Seattle-eligible, Bay Area/SF-required, or includes relocation/sign-on support. No public evidence found for cooldown, tax gross-up, repayment clawback, or vest-cliff terms.

benefits.401k_match: unknown — public sources indicate Databricks offers a 401(k), but I could not verify the employer-match formula from an authoritative public source.
benefits.healthcare: Databricks publicly advertises health benefits, but plan carriers, PPO/HDHP tiers, employer-paid premium percentage, and network breadth are not fully disclosed publicly.
benefits.dental_vision: Dental and vision coverage appear to be offered according to Databricks careers/benefits and third-party benefits pages, but plan-level details are not public.
benefits.estimated_copay: unknown — cannot estimate Mitchell's monthly out-of-pocket without the 2026 employee premium table, plan tier, dependents, and WA/CA enrollment options.
benefits.meals_provided: unknown — Databricks offices are reported to have office perks/snacks/food in some locations [UNVERIFIED], but I could not verify current 2026 breakfast/lunch/dinner policy by office.
benefits.mental_health: unknown — Databricks public materials reference wellness/employee support generally, but I could not verify provider, session count, or dependent coverage.
benefits.other_perks: Publicly described perks include PTO/flexible time off, parental/family benefits, learning/career development, equity, and wellness-style benefits; exact 2026 allowances and eligibility are not publicly verified.

sentiment.blind_score: unknown — TeamBlind company pages and recent discussion quality are difficult to verify without logged-in access; historical public snippets tend to frame Databricks as high-comp, high-growth, and intense [UNVERIFIED].
sentiment.glassdoor_score: Glassdoor public company page should be checked live for the current rating; recurring public-review themes historically include strong technical talent, strong compensation/equity upside, rapid growth, and pace/process strain [UNVERIFIED].
sentiment.reddit_pulse: unknown — I could not verify specific r/cscareerquestions or r/ExperiencedDevs threads from the last 90 days. Recommended searches: Google `site:reddit.com/r/cscareerquestions Databricks culture 2026` and `site:reddit.com/r/ExperiencedDevs Databricks offer equity`.
sentiment.x_pulse: unknown — check recent posts from Databricks official accounts and executives for AI/agentic hiring, product launches, and headcount signals; I could not verify specific 2026 culture or attrition posts.
